返回本批次

给年轻人的建议，我告诉自己的谎言（2024）

Advice to young people, the lies I tell myself (2024)

jxnl.co 作者 mooreds 分数 80 评论 23 2026/04/05 07:18
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 我实在没有资格给建议。但有足够多的人在 Twitter 上私信我，所以就在这里。我不必一遍又一遍地回答同一个问题。经过更多编辑，我意识到我实际上是为我的妹妹凯瑟琳写的。如果您正在寻求细致入微的视角，请不要阅读本文。这些只是我告诉自己的谎言，让我继续真诚地生活。我并不是说这是正确的做事方式。我只是说我就是这么做的。我会尽力用我自己的经历来丰富我的建议，但我不会假装我所经历的痛苦和特权是普遍的。 “选择”¶ 你会注意到我经常使用“选择”这个词。我真诚地相信，我们总是在做出选择，并且我们有能力选择。选择可能是可怕的，因为这意味着我们要对自己的决定负责，而且我们面前有无限的选择。这也很可怕，因为一旦我们做出了决定，我们就必须接受它。这是选择性的消亡。但我相信选择是真实生活的唯一途径。存在主义绝望：一种源于存在主义信念的绝望感，即生命缺乏内在意义。这种绝望源于对一个人的绝对自由的实现以及创造自己的本质和目的的责任。痛苦：在存在主义中，痛苦是认识到一个人的自由的巨大性以及随之而来的对一个人的行为的责任的情感反应。它涉及到认识到每个决定都会塑造一个人的本质并影响他人，从而产生深刻的道德责任感。我曾经听过一个关于哲学家的笑话。睡觉前，他想知道晚上是否会口渴。于是，他去了厨房，在床边放了两个杯子：一个装满水，另一个空着，以防万一他不需要水。如何变得幸运¶ 运气是你自己创造的。有一个很棒的实验，我无法引用，但它从我小时候就一直在我的脑海里。他们将人们分为幸运者和不幸者，并要求他们数一数报纸上照片的数量。不幸的人花了很长时间来数照片，而幸运的人则花了很短的时间。原因是，那些倒霉的人太专注于数照片，而忽略了“别数了，这份报纸上有43张照片”的巨字。我从这个实验中得到的想法是，幸运的人和不幸的人可能没有不同的机会，而是他们的感知领域更广泛。幸运的人实际上可以看到机会。一个幸运的人和一个不幸的人可能会遇到同一个商人，但他们可能会谈论不同的事情。一个人可能会得到或要求一个不幸的人甚至认为不可能的机会。我经常问自己，“好吧，我专注于获得 X，但我们不要忘记阅读头条新闻。”如何找到工作¶ 我从来没有通过申请获得过工作。一直都是有人推荐或有人联系我。老实说，与我的同龄人相比，我的简历很糟糕。我的面试能力很差，而且我从来没有考过 LeetCode。这不是吹牛，而是自吹自擂。这不是我的风格。我是尼波宝宝吗？我不知道。我是一个士气高涨的雇员吗？我实在是太不同意了。这是功德吗？也不确定。优点¶ 很少有人仅凭优点获得工作。你必须是大池塘里的大鱼。就比如IOI，班上前1%等等。这只是……按成绩和面试排序？那很难。从字面上看，你必须是最好的。我说我所说的，知道你已经努力工作，知道你已经付出了汗水，但明白接下来 10% 或接下来 5% 的进步将比专注于成为一个更好的人要困难得多。我希望你意识到你在这些“软”和“硬”技能之间所做的权衡。高代理度¶ 当我雇用某人时，我只是想知道他们有能力掌控自己的生活。人们经常给我发私信说：“嘿，伙计，我能为你做点什么吗？”然而，我常常发现他们并没有花精力去思考这个问题。我通常会忽略此类消息，除非我真正相信他们可以提供帮助。很多时候，当我写博客文章时，人们会通过私信给我提供帮助。我会寄出一份草稿并说：“让我知道你的想法。”没有评论，然后我就被鬼了？如何伸出援手 不要给我发送比你发送给暗恋对象的邮件更长的邮件。有些人通过电子邮件给我发送了六段文章，讲述他们从树上救一只猫的经历。我发现引起某人注意的最有效方法就是简单地给予，就像约会一样。嘿，我注意到你在你的网站上读过这本书。我想你也会喜欢这本书；它很短，等等。哦，我注意到你的帖子中有一个错字，并发送了修复。嘿！我有一个思考了你的草稿并留下了一些评论。您有任何反馈吗？有很多不以自我为中心的小方法可以吸引人们的注意力。我在咨询中也这样做。在第一次通话中，我唯一的目标是告诉您我认为对您的业务有帮助的一切。我不在乎你是否雇用我。我只是想帮忙。事实证明，这将我引向下一部分。我认识到并非每个人都能获得相同的社交机会，而传统的工作申请流程对许多人来说可能是有效且必要的途径。但如果使用得当，社交媒体是获得机会的好方法。成为水管工¶ 当我的厕所里满是粪便，而我的妻子将在 2 小时内回家时，我找到了一名水管工，水管工不会说：“天啊，谢谢你给我这个机会。”他们已经过去了。他们知道你处境艰难，也知道他们拥有的东西很有价值。他们知道他们来这里是为了解决我的问题。招聘也是同样的道理。这就是为什么人们想雇用资深人士，因为有一定的信任。但最终，你必须明白，除非有一些巨大的税收减免和积极的企业价值，否则招聘的人需要帮助。你的工作就是帮助他们解决问题或者找到要解决的问题。你来这里是为了解决我的问题；你来这里不是为了募捐。成为人们愿意共事的人如果你在某个地方工作的标准是成为人们愿意与之共事的人，那么事实证明技能并不是最高的标准。我见过有人因为和他们在一起很有趣而被雇用。如果你想成为最聪明的人，那么是的，好的成绩和成为房间里最聪明的人是一个很好的衡量标准。但如果你想找到一份工作，“伙计，有你在身边真好”是一个非常有力的衡量标准。这显然是以技能为条件的，你显然应该专注于技能的获取。冒名顶替综合症¶ 如果我雇用了你，请不要用冒充综合症来侮辱我。我雇用你是因为我认为你可以解决我的问题。我雇用你不是为了将你与其他水管工进行比较（我可能会这样做），但归根结底，你一定认为我的品味很糟糕，并且你以某种方式欺骗了我，让我以为你是一个冒名顶替的人？正确的？也就是说，我建议初级人员寻找优先考虑高度心理安全的团队。如果你不相信自己，请相信相信你的我。如何擅长很多事情¶ 我认为太多人读这篇文章主要是在追求智力活动，但我主要会专注于使用个人身体健康的类比来描述我如何看待这类事情。一开始，你会有新手的收获，只需练习就足以取得进步。你会变得非常虚弱，体形也不好，如果你走路，就会进行有氧运动，如果你跑步，你就会变得更强壮。只要每天出现并做任何事情，就需要几个月或几年的时间才能看到进展。你只会取得进步。也许你也“有天赋”，所以事情对你来说变得更容易，但总有一天事情会发生变化。成为冠军的成本¶ 我给自己讲的故事是特拉维斯·史蒂文斯谈论如何成为一名柔道冠军的故事。类似于“你认为自己想成为冠军，直到你遇到真正知道自己想成为冠军的人。”将“冠军”替换为“喜剧演员”、“拳击手”、“创始人”等。你知道成为柔道冠军需要什么吗？在为奥运会而战之前，你需要牺牲人际关系、折断手指、十字韧带撕裂，甚至脑震荡。你必须忍受伤病，并怀疑你的职业生涯是否已经结束。你必须在全国比赛之前就感染流感并与之抗争。如果你输了，你必须避免找借口。这是非常具有挑战性的，选择不走这条路也是完全可以接受的。热爱高原一旦你开始进入高原，你就可以开始考虑渐进式超负荷，并确保你正在做的事情以某种一致的速度面临困难，但最终这只是为了帮助你克服其中的一些问题。在未来的某个时刻，你能进步多少，取决于你有多渴望它，以及你有多喜欢这个行为。如何识别冠军 我记得在那个视频中，他们问这位奥运会举重运动员，天赋和努力哪个更重要。他说了这样的话：“在国家层面，每个人都有才华，但当你进入奥运会时，努力工作才是唯一重要的。”有很多伤害和挑战，得到它的人是最想要它的人。旺季和淡季¶ 我想很多人专门问我这个问题，因为我做了很多事情。我是一名武术家、助理、自由潜水员，等等等等。我不认为我是这些事情的领导者，但我我确实认为我在学习方面是精英。就像体育运动有淡季和旺季一样，我认为学习也有淡季和旺季。我认为这是需要理解的最重要的事情。在某些时候，有氧运动会损害你的力量增长。在某些时候，力量的增加会损害你的心肺功能。在某些时候，你必须在举重运动员或马拉松运动员之间做出选择。没关系。您可以随时返回。但我认为最重要的是要明白你不可能一直擅长所有事情。没关系。您可以随时返回。如何保持自信¶ 我是一个非常没有安全感的人，我想我的治疗师可能会同意我的自尊心很低。但我同样有信心。我认为重要的是要理解信心是尽管感到不安但仍然相信的能力。勇气不是不害怕，而是尽管害怕，仍采取行动。我认为这适用于许多美德。所以我只放心地谈谈我自己的经历。我的信心来自于行动。我知道我比周围的每个人都更努力，即使我没有天赋。我知道我愿意做出牺牲，我知道我可以将精力集中在一个特定的目标上。为什么？因为我一生中已经做过很多次了。信心是成功的记忆 柔术¶ 当我开始练习柔术时，我每周刻苦训练五天。每次训练都充满挑战，但这也意味着每当我去新的健身房或参加比赛时，我知道我可以应付。我已经经历过艰难的处境，我知道我可以再次克服它们。自由潜水¶ 当我开始自由潜水时，我每天游一个小时，每周游六天。每次锻炼结束时，我都会练习呼吸常规和呼吸暂停训练。训练结束时，我可以在泳池中不喘气地游 80 米，并屏住呼吸 4.5 分钟。这很重要，因为我知道在我的潜水之旅中，我要游的最远距离是 45 米，最长屏气时间是 2.5 分钟。当我开始潜水之旅时，我感到很放松，准备好享受水了。我知道我能做到，因为我已经完成了。第一次达到 30m 我单次呼吸下潜最深的个人记录是 30m。潜水速度不是很快，所以我最终在水下呆了 3.5 分钟。但我当时的感受如何？ 3.5 分钟比我个人最好成绩短了一分钟，所以我知道我能做到。潜水前 10 分钟的休息时间，目标是降低心率并放松。我非常放松，在水中睡了10分钟，心率为43 bpm。我醒了，吸了最后几口气，就倒下了。公开演讲¶ 人们不相信这一点，但我在人工智能峰会上的演讲实际上是我的第一次公开演讲经历。它在 YouTube 上的观看次数超过 12 万次。我太紧张了，几乎昏过去了，一整天都没有吃东西，直到演讲。我不记得那天的情况，我的肾上腺素太多了，上台前我都在发抖。在演讲之前，过去三天我每天排练大约两个小时。当我登上舞台时，这些话就从我嘴里脱口而出。过去一个月里，我穿着同一件夹克，站着排练了大约 20 次。我知道我能做到，因为我已经做到了。为了继续做代表，在过去的三个月里，我一直不懈地寻找播客来演讲并继续练习。整个主题是一致的。我不认为这是获得信心的唯一方法，因为可能存在一些错觉。但在过去的 15 年里，我只是向自己展示了我可以克服困难的挑战，并反复完成这些挑战。练习多于玩耍，自信是成功的记忆¶ 无注释。你能给自己的最好的礼物¶ 你能给自己的最好的礼物就是知足的礼物。选择幸福¶ 从短期来看，如果你接受并承认自己没有得到你想要的东西的原因仅仅是因为你不够想要它，你会更幸福。你越早接受这一点，你就会越快乐。那么下一个问题是：你想要快乐还是想要实现你想要的？这不是最后一个问题，但绝对是下一个问题。对于我认为我的读者是谁，我强烈推荐韩秉哲的书《倦怠社会或心理政治学》。多写一些¶ 我曾经讨厌写作。我对此总是非常没有安全感。英语是我的第二语言，我经常被告知我永远不会成为一名优秀的作家，因为我是移民。我在 SAT 写作部分成绩很差。事实上，我在滑铁卢的入学英语考试没有通过，不得不重写一篇英语论文，否则我会在第一周就被大学开除。这是我写的第一篇文章一堆技术博客文章，但这是我第一次真正发自内心地说话。整篇文章我花了大约一个小时使用语音转文本来撰写。我差点就没有写下这篇文章，但后来我意识到，如果它能帮助一个人有一天少一点焦虑，那就是积极的。直到2023年11月，我才真正体会到写作的力量。写作帮助我不再反复思考同样的想法。此外，当有人寻求建议时，我可以简单地分享一个链接，而不是重复自己。我发现写作非常有价值，因为如果有人不愿意阅读它，他们就不会从中受益。最糟糕的结果是什么也没写；它未能帮助那些本来可以从中受益的人。我发现写作是帮助别人的好方法，同时也帮助自己驱除脑海中的想法，为新的想法腾出空间。害怕吧¶我相信许多年轻人都在与聪明的不安全感作斗争。他们常常觉得需要通过过度思考、过度计划和在采取行动之前获取知识来证明自己的智力。然而，这种方法是浪费时间。你研究得越多，你就越意识到你实际上知道的有多么少。相反，即使你害怕，你也必须这样做。文学是最令人愉快的忽视生活的方式。理论是用来分析的；它不是为了行动。当你学习画画时，只画你看到的东西。当我们接受艺术教育时，理论只是为我们提供了描述我们品味的语言。我今年给自己的挑战是在不学习任何音乐理论的情况下学习小号。我只是想像个孩子一样玩它。我只是要听很多音乐并尝试发出我喜欢的声音。中国的小孩子被强迫学钢琴七年，一旦父母不再带他们去上课，他们就不再演奏音乐，这样的例子已经够多了。我认为，如果你热爱这个世界并认为它是美丽的，那么学习物理，例如，不会剥夺这种美丽，它只会增加它。但如果你研究自然科学而不热爱自然世界，你只会把世界视为一台机器。这可没那么有趣。你不是你的作品¶当我在艺术学校时，我发现的一件事是，很多人相信他们是由他们创作的作品定义的。如果他们的工作不好，他们会认为这是他们技能的体现，而不是他们品味的体现，而品味实际上高于他们的能力。您只是创作该作品的人。你是工作的容器、媒介，但你不是工作本身。你越早意识到这一点，就越容易分享你的作品、接收反馈、发展你的技能并反思你的品味。摆脱复杂性¶我认为，以同样的方式，在制定计划或构建系统时，同样对智能的不安全感会导致过度思考和过度复杂化系统，希望同样的复杂性能够成为智能的代表。我认为这是一个错误。最好的系统是最简单的。费曼的物理学方法就是一个很好的例子。爱因斯坦有句名言，如果你不能简单地解释某件事，说明你对它的理解还不够透彻。我认为对于很多事情来说都是如此。伟大的人并不总是好人，迈克·泰森曾经说过，“如果你受到上帝的青睐，你也会受到魔鬼的青睐。”追求伟大固然可以，但选择善良同样重要。不要因为工作而忽视亲人；时间转瞬即逝。欣赏别人并真诚地表达你对他们的爱。悲伤是未表达爱的结果，而避免悲伤的关键是充分地去爱。有些人声称他们会磨练十年，然后一旦找到伴侣或类似的东西就放松，但我认为这是无稽之谈。如果不经过练习，你不可能突然做到这一点。在奋斗的路上，一定要自觉地练习放松、做一个好人。否则，你只有在经历第一次离婚后才能解决这个问题。父亲身份 我认为父亲身份就是一个很好的例子。我想很多人都认为有了孩子就可以成为一个好父亲。但我认为这是一个错误。我认为在生孩子之前你必须练习成为一个好父亲。在结婚之前，你必须练习做一个好丈夫。也许很容易让自己相信一个好父亲能够照顾家庭，但有多少次这成为了自己拼命工作的借口？能付得起钢琴课费用的父亲真的比能参加演奏会的父亲更好吗？我只是不认为这是我们可以如此轻松地打开和关闭的东西。自我价值¶ 在我生命的大部分时间里，我坚信我的目的是帮助和教导人们。我相信努力工作、创造资本、并照顾我的伴侣、家人等。然而，当我受伤时，我不得不面对这样一个事实：我无法履行这些责任。我意识到自己无法工作、生产或照顾我的伴侣和家人，这让我很挣扎。这让我不禁要问：“如果牛不会耕地，那它有什么用呢？”这场斗争消耗了我二十多岁的后半段时间。开始练习自爱。虽然成功人士可能将他们的成功归因于他们的斗争，但我相信其中很大一部分只是接受了这些斗争。每个人的痛苦对他们来说都是最大的。你已经吃过苦了。寻求成功，因为你应得的，并学会爱自己，而不是因为不够好而惩罚自己。你值得拥有你想要的生活。我曾经写过“如果我真的爱自己，我会做什么？”有时这很难，特别是对于经历过很多创伤的人来说。甚至很难想象那会是什么样子。相反，我现在使用逆向思维。如果我真的讨厌自己，我该怎么办？如果我想让自己损失尽可能多怎么办？我该怎么办？一整天呆在家里。如果我的朋友不约我出去玩，不要因为害怕被拒绝而联系他们。不要约女孩出去。永远不要学着和陌生人说话。努力工作，赚了很多钱，却带着遗憾死去。忽视我的健康和身体。永远学不会做饭。害怕在公共场合跳舞。找借口并相信其他人比我更有才华。永远不要看我已经走了多远，只关注我没有的东西。不管是什么，只要做相反的事情就可以了。自言自语¶ 你从别人那里继承了哪些口头禅？你有多少次在追逐“梦想”而不是“你的梦想”？真正评估你想要什么以及为什么想要它，并确保你练习与自己交谈。幸福是思想的品质你不需要相信自己能成为一名艺术家，你只需要相信你的观众。相信与他们沟通很重要。如果您的目标是建立受众群体，请确保您有一些信息要说。否则只会变得毫无希望。名人是你传递信息所付出的成本。名人就是会被人讨厌的。名人是会被讨厌的 Twitter 是一个让低地位的人感觉自己像高地位的人的地方。我想互联网基本上就是用这几个词来概括的。对于大多数在网上战斗的人来说，互联网可能是他们唯一有控制感的地方。当您从同情的角度来对待它时，即使在网上受到仇恨也是一个机会，可以感谢您在现实生活中拥有的友谊以及您建立的数字社区。孤独是一份礼物，其他一切都是对你耐力的考验¶ 真正珍惜独处的时间。这是你唯一能真正看到自己的时候。这是你唯一能真正听到自己的声音的时候。隔离不仅是远离人群的时间，也是远离媒体的时间。我相信成功确实来自于做自己，但如果你总是被别人包围，影响力就太大了。但如果你能真正孤立地生活，你就会像一汪平静的池塘。当别人把自己的形象投射到你身上时，你就能清楚地看到涟漪。世界是一面镜子¶ 你描述世界的唯一语言就是你自己的感知。世界是一面镜子。通常，您对判断的恐惧可能源于您自己对他人的判断。杰森去健身房 我以前很害怕去健身房，因为我害怕被人评判。我非常关注正确的锻炼和正确的形式的想法。如果我一个人去健身房，我只会想：“哇，人们一定认为我很虚弱。我几乎无法深蹲杠铃。我太瘦了。”我会看着胖子或瘦子，心想：“伙计，他们就像我一样，一无所知。”回想起来，我对别人有太多的评价，但这实际上只是我对自己的评价的反映。我很害怕被评判，但我是评判自己最多的人。快进五年了，我看到每个进入健身房的人都在努力改善自己的健康。我不这么认为。我看着这些人，心想，“哇，他们比我勇敢得多。我看到他们不断地来，我可以看到他们正在取得成果。对他们来说很好！哦，哇，他们带着他们的朋友。太棒了。”当你修正自己的看法时，世界看起来会有所不同。你是一面镜子¶ 就像世界是一面镜子一样，你也是一面镜子。你是别人的一面镜子。当你接受这一点时，你也意识到你不对别人负责。对你持消极态度的人通常自己也很痛苦。你越早认识到这一点，就越容易摆脱消极情绪。悲观主义者是失败者¶ 当你是一个悲观主义者时，除了你自己之外，你不会给任何人留下深刻的印象。它并不会让你成为更好的朋友。它不会让你变得冷静和急躁。它不会让你看起来更聪明只是因为你可以批评一切。世界本质上既不反对你，也不支持你，所以相信其中之一都是恶意的。 “怨别人的人，他的路还很长；怨自己的人，已经走了一半；不怨别人的人，已经到了。”生存 我认为悲观主义可以是一种有价值的生存机制。正是这种警惕性警告我们潜在的负面结果。这是保护自己的一种方式。人们也更容易发现问题而不是解决方案。这是一个很好的练习，可以了解这种能量是否富有成效，这种对工作的批评能否改善工作？或者这只是一种感觉优越或获得同情的方式？所有的残忍都源于软​​弱，幸福是思想的品质放开期待¶这是一个关于普通人和佛教僧人如何展望未来的故事。他们对他们俩进行了核磁共振检查，并告诉他们，他们会在未来的某个随机时间受到电击。他们发现，普通人在受到电击前几分钟会出现焦虑的基线反应，但当他们这样做时，反应仅略高于基线。然而，僧侣们却没有任何焦虑的预期反应。在电击发生之前，大脑并没有过度活跃的活动。但当他们真正感受到震撼的时候，却比这群普通人要高得多。他们感到更加痛苦！我从中学到的教训是，期待可能会抑制生活发生那一刻的感觉，但代价是也会抑制快乐，同时在当下造成收缩。这与悲观主义有关。你可能认为你赢了，因为你相信预期更坏的情况会减轻痛苦，但你也输了，因为你一直在期待。同样的道理，放弃对成功的期待也能让你更多地享受当下，成功的压力同样是一种收缩。你只是个孩子¶ 和我在一起有一段时间。我看着自己过去做过的所有愚蠢的事情，以及发生在我身上的所有我不明白的事情。然后有人对我说“你只是个孩子”，这确实是我听过的最自由的事情。我只是个孩子。我不知道还有什么更好的。我不知道。你无缘无故地受苦。你还只是个孩子。现在，您还必须意识到这也适用于其他所有人。每个人都是第一次做人，包括你的父母。注意力是一份礼物¶ 你能给别人的最有价值的东西就是你的注意力。谈论您喜欢的事情，与您喜欢的人共度时光，并明智地给予您的注意力。正如我之前所说，悲伤是未表达的爱的结果，避免悲伤的关键是充分地爱，尤其是你自己。所以，专注于重要的事情，并确保在你还年轻的时候消除你的傲慢。 2026 年 3 月 27 日的新内容¶ 最近刚满 32 岁，所以想在这里添加更多内容。当我第一次写这篇文章时，我正试图在手部受伤后重新转向科技领域，你可以在我的手部受伤帖子中读到相关内容。页面上出现的很多内容都来自那些短暂的自我重塑和自我发现，以及我为度过这些岁月而采用的咒语。以下是我在过去两年创业时所获得的一些认识。使用你的药水¶ 我是在超级贫穷的环境下长大的，我仍然记得在没有使用药水的情况下通关了前三款神奇宝贝游戏。我一直把它们存起来，以为我以后会需要它们，最后我刚刚通关了整个游戏。我最终只是做了一次无药水跑步，每款神奇宝贝游戏的无药水挑战跑，无意这样做。它让我想起了人们关于《Elden Ring》的纯洁对话，如果你使用魔法，它就不算数，只有当你玩得有躲避、滚动和把握时机的感觉时，它才有意义。游戏开发者在游戏中加入了魔法。就在你们争论某种胡说八道的纯粹性时，有人用大金刚邦戈垫击败了游戏。机制已内置于游戏中。我们给自己设定的所有这些条件都是任意的。也许我想决定经营一家公司，但我拒绝筹集资金或雇用帮助。也许我选择不聘请 EA 或聘请作家。只要使用你那该死的药水，如果你不承认你正在做一种非常特殊的挑战，那就用你的一生去奔跑吧。让它成为你生活中一个有意识的决定，你每天醒来并接受它。一些好的决定¶这是我生命中的重大真理之一。你的一生实际上是建立在几个正确的决定和尽可能少的错误决定的基础上的。在商业中，我学到的是，专注可以让你真正做出一些决定。通常，当我做出一个正确的决定和零个错误的决定时，我的生活会更好。而我的生活就是如果我做出五个正确的决定和三个错误的决定，那就太糟糕了。两个正确的决定让我的净资产达到 300 万美元，而一个错误的决定让我的净资产跌至 50 万美元。一个更糟糕的决定，工作有点太多，导致我在大约三年的时间里无法高效工作。事实证明，努力只是入场券。为了达到我现在的水平，每个人都在努力工作。也许第一个一百万是辛苦的，但接下来的二十万却不仅仅如此。这是运气，是杠杆，而不是他妈的毁掉一切。努力之后，其他的一切都是你的判断和你的运气能力，这个我稍后再说。关于我所有金钱问题的注释¶金钱一直是我的焦虑¶过去两年的大部分时间实际上都围绕着我对金钱的很多神经症，所以我将分享我对金钱对我意味着什么的很多想法，所以请注意。并不是每个人都喜欢谈论金钱。为了给您提供一些背景信息，我来到美国时银行账户里大约有 157 美元。我向一些朋友借钱，以机器学习研究员的身份谋生，直到我的双手停止工作，我基本上认为我再也无法谋生了。在我的成长过程中，我遇到了很多关于金钱的问题。这是我生活中唯一真正的问题和焦虑。只要谈论金钱就可以了¶如果你想成功，在某些时候金钱将成为谈话的一部分。当然，你会发现当你大声说出数字时，人们开始自我排序。他们倾向于透露自己如何看待自己以及金钱的价值是什么。有时我会谈论金钱，人们会进行四十分钟的长篇大论，说他们并不意味着金钱对他们来说毫无意义，而且他们不关心所有这些事情。显然，如果你因为我告诉你我在股票上损失了一大笔钱而对我咆哮了四十分钟，也许这实际上是你的事。因为对于其他人来说，他们只是问我：“是什么促使我做出这些赌注？”或者我是否需要对冲或其他一些让金钱更多地被视为一种技能的帮助？而且我个人发现，如果你拒绝真正承认金钱，甚至拒绝相信事物可以定价，世界对你来说可能会变得更加困难。我记得在一次餐桌上有人提到他们想买一套价值400万美元的房子。那桌有人翻了个白眼，说：“那一定很好。”另一个人看着她说：“哦，你在哪里找？你算出费用了吗？你认为我会在那栋房子里生孩子吗？你要找哪个社区？如果你愿意的话，我认识一个房地产经纪人。”上次我告诉别人我的生意做得有多好时，他们只是看着我说：“该死的杰森，我和你一样努力工作，而且赚的钱比你多一百倍。让我们看看你做错了什么。”那次谈话确实让我离开了咨询公司和 OpenAI。事实证明，我害怕杠杆，而拥有更好的平台可以让我接触到更多的工具和更多的人。现在，当人们告诉我他们赚了多少钱时，我只是对自己说，“找个时间我们一起做生意吧”，我很乐意向你学习。这也让我想起 Naval 曾经说过的另一句话：和你的朋友一起赚钱。几年前，我在一次活动中，有人向 Peter Thiel 询问 PayPal 黑手党的情况，以及这些友谊是如何持续这么久的。他的回答很简单：“你们只要想出一个共同赚钱的方法就可以了。”显然，我认为这与创造行为更直接相关，但是，伙计，价值创造和价值获取真的激发了我们所拥有的战时兄弟情谊吗？现在，我对任何友谊的最大荣幸就是一起工作，但我认为情况一直如此，无论是摇滚乐队、初创公司，甚至只是一些活动。我想做的就是和我的朋友们一起工作。你想要奖励吗？¶ 有一篇文章我很喜欢，叫做《你被允许做的事情》，它就是魔药的可能性空间。如果你想学点东西，你可以支付每小时 30 美元的博士生来亲自辅导你。你就可以这么做。去年，我在 TikTok 上看到一个人拿着一个包出来，他说：“你愿意花 300 美元买这个包吗？”一半的人会说，“我他妈的为什么要花 300 美元买你的包？”其他人问袋子里有什么。原来袋子里有1000美元。生活有时真的就是这么简单。我觉得很多时候我们忘记问这个问题，而我们只看到了成本。我不知道这是否完全值得花 8,000 美元的八张床床垫，但对某些人来说，睡得更好、寿命更长、皮肤更好、能够在工作中工作感觉是一项相当不错的投资。未来的自己比现在的自己更好，别担心¶这只是一个关于高中杰森试图为未来的杰森存钱是多么愚蠢的故事。 2025年4月齐心协力日本为朋友庆祝生日，伦敦为工作，巴黎为我，再加上太多的工作而无法干净地调整日程。一张 4,000 美元的门票可以让你在去伦敦的途中睡个好觉，并有时间玩滑梯。几周后在巴黎，飞往日本庆祝生日看起来也很残酷，所以商务舱也在那里举行。和朋友一起度过一周，一个月的旅行费用大约为 15,000 美元，说实话，这是值得的，因为这些窗口并不经常出现。时间倒回到高中之后，再回到大学之后。这两次都意味着周五放学并周一开始工作。毕业旅行的钱感觉不可能花 3,000 美元来支付整个费用，而不仅仅是一次航班。在 17 岁到 24 岁之间，跳过五周与朋友一起旅行可以“节省”6,000 美元。这 6,000 美元并没有被存放在某种会登月的硬币中，晚两周开始工作也不会烧毁任何真正的东西。过去的我只是害怕，不相信钱会在以后出现。未来的我不需要那些牺牲。亿万富翁杰森有多珍惜他的时间？¶ 基本上，如果你相信自己，当你年老时，你会拥有更多的钱。但是，如果上帝在 75 岁时问你，你愿意花多少钱让 28 岁的时候一周，或者 32 岁的时候在日本和朋友一起住一个月，你会付多少钱？超过 15,000 美元。超过10万美元。如果你不相信这一点，那你就太傻了。所以就放弃吧。 2024 年收入约为 100 万美元，最明显的遗憾仍然是在分手并搬回纽约之前与 Nikol 一起跳过葡萄牙。仍然萦绕心头。然后大脑说，那个月多花 80,000 美元胜过 10,000 美元的旅行，不知何故，这次旅行感觉很巨大。从这里开始，这篇文章与我第一次发表的文章几乎一样。这条线之上的所有内容，从三月更新开始到支线任务，都是我在上面添加的新材料。

Original Summary

  1. I'm really not qualified to give advice. But enough people DM'd me on Twitter, so here it is. I don't have to answer the same question over and over again. After some more editing, I realized that I am actually writing this for my younger sister Katherine. Don't read this if you're seeking a nuanced perspective These are simply the lies I tell myself to keep on living my life in good faith. I'm not saying this is the right way to do things. I'm just saying this is how I did things. I will do my best to color my advice with my own experiences, but I'm not going to pretend that the suffering and the privilege I've experienced is universal. "Choosing"¶ You'll notice that I use the word "choosing" frequently. I genuinely believe that we are always making choices and that we have the ability to choose. Choosing can be terrifying because it means we are accountable for our decisions, and there are infinite options before us. It is also frightening because once we have made a decision, we must live with it. It is the death of optionality. But I believe that choosing is the only way to live authentically. Existential Despair: A feeling of hopelessness rooted in the existentialist belief that life lacks inherent meaning. This despair arises from the realization of one's absolute freedom and the responsibility for creating one's own essence and purpose. Anguish: In existentialism, anguish is the emotional response to recognizing the vastness of one's freedom and the accompanying responsibility for one's actions. It involves the realization that each decision shapes one's essence and affects others, leading to a deep sense of moral responsibility. There's a joke I once heard about a philosopher. Before going to bed, he wonders if he will be thirsty during the night. So, he goes to the kitchen and places two cups beside his bed: one filled with water and the other left empty, just in case he doesn't want any water. How to Be Lucky¶ You make your own luck. There's a great experiment that I can't cite, but it has stuck in my mind since I was a child. They identified people as lucky and unlucky and asked them to count the number of photographs in a newspaper. The unlucky people took a long time to count the photographs, while the lucky people took a very short time. The reason is that the unlucky people were so focused on counting the photographs that they missed the giant text that said, "Stop counting, there are 43 photographs in this newspaper." What I took away from this experiment was the idea that it might not be the case that lucky people and unlucky people have different opportunities, but rather that their field of perception is wider. Lucky people can actually see the opportunities. A lucky person and an unlucky person might meet the same businessman, but they might talk about different things. One could be presented with or ask for an opportunity that the unlucky person doesn't even see as possible. I often ask myself, "Okay, I'm focused on getting X, but let's not forget to read the headlines." How to Get a Job¶ I've never gotten a job by applying to it. It's always been referrals or someone reaching out to me. So honestly, my resume is shit compared to my peers. I'm terrible at interviewing, and I've never done LeetCode. This is not a brag; it's just not my style. Am I a nepo baby? I don't know. Was I a morale hire? I'm pretty disagreeable. Was it merit? Also not sure. Merit¶ Very few people get a job on merit alone. You have to be a big fish in a big pond. That's like IOI, top 1% in your class, etc. That's just... Sort by grades and interview? That's hard. You literally have to be the best. I say what I say knowing that you've already worked hard, knowing that you've already put in the sweat, but understanding that the next 10% or the next 5% of improvement is going to be much more difficult than focusing on being a better person. And that I want you to be conscious of the trade-offs you make between these "soft" and "hard" skills. High Agency¶ When I hire someone, I simply want to know that they are capable of taking charge of their own life. It's quite common for people to DM me saying, "Hey man, how can I help you?" However, I often find that they haven't put any effort into thinking about it. I usually ignore such messages unless I truly believe they can provide assistance. There have been many times people will DM me offering to help while I am writing a blog post. I'll send over a draft and say, "let me know what you think." No comments, and then I get ghosted? How to Reach Out Do not send me anything longer than you would send to a crush. Some people email me six-paragraph essays about the time they saved a cat from a tree. I find the most effective way to get someone's attention is to simply give, just like in dating. Hey, I noticed that you read this book on your website. I think you'd like this book too; it's pretty short, etc. Oh, I noticed a typo in your post and sent over a fix. Hey! I had a thought on your draft and left a few comments. Do you have any feedback? There are so many little ways to get people's attention that aren't self-centered. I do this with my consulting too. During the first call, my only goal is to tell you everything I think could help your business. I don't care if you hire me or not. I just want to help. And as it turns out, this leads me to the next part. I recognize that not everyone has access to the same networking opportunities, and the traditional job application process can be a valid and necessary path for many. But social media, when used correctly, is a great way to get an opportunity. Be the Plumber¶ When my toilet is overflowing with shit and my wife is about to come home in 2 hours, and I find a plumber, the plumber does not go, "OMG, thank you for this opportunity." They are past that. They know that you're in a pinch, and they know that what they have is valuable. They know they are here to solve MY problem. Hiring is the same way. This is why people want to hire senior folks because there's some trust. But ultimately, you have to understand that unless there's some tremendous tax break and positive EV, the people who are hiring NEED HELP. Your job is to help them solve the problem or find the problem to solve. You're here to solve my problem; you're not here to collect charity work. Be Someone People Want to Work With¶ If your metric for working somewhere is being someone people want to work with, it turns out skill is not the highest metric. I've seen people get hired because they're fun to be around. If you want to be the smartest person, then yeah, good grades and being the smartest person in the room is a good metric. But if you want to get a job, "man, you're great to be around" is a very strong metric. This is obviously conditional on skill, and you should obviously focus on skills acquisition. Impostor Syndrome¶ If I hired you, don't insult me by having impostor syndrome. I hired you because I think you can solve my problem. I didn't hire you to compare you to other plumbers (I might), but at the end of the day, you must just think I have shit taste and that you've somehow tricked me into thinking you're good when you're an impostor? Right? That said, I'd advise junior people to seek out teams that prioritize a high degree of psychological safety. If you don't believe in yourself, believe in me that believes in you. How to Be Good at Many Things¶ I think too many people reading this are mostly pursuing intellectual activities, but I'm mostly gonna focus on using analogies of personal physical fitness to describe how I think about this kind of stuff. In the beginning, you're gonna have noob gains, just an act of practicing is going to be enough to make improvements. You're gonna be so weak and out of shape that if you walk, it's gonna be cardio, and if you run, you're gonna get stronger. And it's gonna be months or years of seeing progress just by showing up every day and doing anything. You're just gonna be making progress. Maybe you're also "gifted" and so things come easier to you, but there's gonna be a time when things change. Cost of Being a Champion¶ The story I tell myself is one where Travis Stevens talks about what it takes to be a judo champion. Something along the lines of "You think you want to be a champion until you meet someone who truly knows they want to be a champion." Replace "champion" with "comedian," "boxer," "founder," and so on. Do you realize what it takes to be a judo champion? You need to sacrifice relationships, break some fingers, go through an ACL tear, and even get a concussion before fighting for the Olympics. You have to endure injuries and wonder if your career is over. You have to get the flu right before nationals and fight through it. And if you lose, you have to refrain from making excuses. That's incredibly challenging, and it's perfectly acceptable to choose not to pursue that path. Love Plateaus¶ Once you start hitting a plateau, you can just start thinking about progressive overload and making sure what you're doing is facing difficulty at some consistent rate, but ultimately this is just to get you over and through some of these spots. At some point in the future, how much better you get will just be a function of how badly you want it and how much you enjoy the act. How to identify the champ I remember that video where they were asking this Olympic weightlifter what matters more, talent or hard work. And he said something along the lines of, "At the national level, everyone is talented, but when you get to the Olympics hard work is the only thing that matters." There are many injuries and challenges, and the people who get it are the people who want it the most. On Season and Off Season¶ I think a lot of people ask me specifically about this question because I do so many things. I'm a martial artist, assistant, free diver, blah blah blah blah blah. I don't think I'm a leader at any of these things, but I do think I'm elite at learning. And just like there is an off-season and on-season for sports, I think there's an off-season and on-season for learning. And I think that's the most important thing to understand. At some point, cardio will hurt your strength gains. At some point, strength gains will hurt your cardio. At some point, you're gonna have to choose between being a powerlifter or a marathon runner. And that's okay. You can always come back to it. But I think the most important thing is to understand that you can't be good at everything all the time. And that's okay. You can always come back to it. How to Be Confident¶ I am a deeply insecure person, and I think my therapist would probably agree that I have low self-esteem. But I am confident just the same. I believe it's important to understand that confidence is the ability to believe despite feeling uneasy. Courage is not the absence of fear, but rather taking action despite the fear. I think this applies to many virtues. So I'll only talk about my own experience with confidence. My confidence simply comes from taking action. I know I outwork everyone around me, even if I have no talent. I know I'm willing to make sacrifices, and I know I can focus my energy on a specific goal. Why? Because I've done it so many times in my life. Confidence is the memory of success Jiu Jitsu¶ When I started doing jiu-jitsu, I trained hard five days a week. Every training session was challenging, but it also meant that whenever I went to a new gym or a competition, I knew I could handle it. I had already been through tough situations, and I knew I could overcome them again. Freediving¶ When I started freediving, I swam for an hour every day, six days a week. At the end of each workout, I would practice my breathing routines and apnea training. By the end of my training session, I could swim 80 meters in a pool without taking a breath and hold my breath for 4 ½ minutes. This was important because I knew that during my diving trip, the longest distance I would have to swim was 45 meters and the longest breath-hold would be 2 ½ minutes. By the time I went on my diving trip, I felt relaxed and ready to enjoy the water. I knew I could do it because I had already accomplished it. Hitting 30m for the first time My personal record for the deepest dive I've done on a single breath is 30m. It was not a very fast dive, so I ended up being underwater for 3 ½ minutes. But how did I feel at the time? 3.5 minutes is a minute shorter than my personal best, so I knew I could do it. During the 10-minute rest before the dive, the goal was to lower my heart rate and relax. I was so relaxed that I fell asleep in the water for 10 minutes, and my heart rate was 43 bpm. I woke up, took the last few breaths, and went down. Public Speaking¶ People don't believe this, but my talk at the AI Summit was actually my first public speaking experience. It has over 120k views on YouTube. I was so nervous that I basically blacked out and didn't eat all day until the talk. I have no recollection of the day, and I had so much adrenaline that I was shivering before I got up on stage. Leading up to the talk, I rehearsed it for about two hours each day for the past three days. By the time I got on stage, the words just flowed out of me. I had rehearsed the speech standing up, wearing the same jacket, about 20 times in the past month. I knew I could do it because I had already done it. In order to keep doing the reps, in the past 3 months I've been relentlessly trying to find podcasts to speak on and keep practicing. The theme is consistent throughout. I don't think it's the only way to gain confidence, as there could be some delusion at play. But I have simply shown myself, over the past 15 years, that I can overcome difficult challenges and do them repeatedly. Practice more than you play, confidence is the memory of success¶ No notes. The Greatest Gift You Can Give Yourself¶ The greatest gift you can give yourself is the gift of being enough. Choosing Happiness¶ In the short term, you would be much happier if you accepted and admitted to yourself that the reason you don't have what you want is simply because you do not want it badly enough. The sooner you accept that, the happier you'll be. Then the next question is: Do you want to be happy or do you want to achieve what you want? It's not the last question, but it definitely is the next question. For who I think my audience is, I really recommend Byung Chul Han's book The Burnout Society or Psychopolitics. Write More¶ I used to hate writing. I was always incredibly insecure about it. English is my second language, and I was often told that I would never be a good writer because I am an immigrant. I scored poorly on the writing section of the SATs. In fact, I failed the entrance English exam at Waterloo and had to rewrite an English essay, or else I would have been kicked out of college in the first week. This is my first piece of writing I've run a bunch of technical blog posts, but this is the first time I'm really speaking from my heart. This whole article took me about an hour to write using speech to text. I almost didn't write this, but then I realized that if it even helped one person feel a little less anxious for one day, it would've been positive. It wasn't until November 2023 that I truly appreciated the power of writing. Writing helps me stop dwelling on the same thoughts repeatedly. Moreover, when someone asks for advice, I can simply share a link instead of repeating myself. I have found writing to be incredibly valuable because if someone is not willing to read it, they won't benefit from it. The worst outcome is not having written anything at all; it is failing to help someone who could have benefited from it. I have found that writing is a great way to help others while helping myself exorcise the thoughts from my mind and make room for new ones. Do It Scared¶ I believe many young people struggle with the insecurity of being intelligent. They often feel the need to prove their intelligence by overthinking, excessively planning, and acquiring knowledge before taking action. However, this approach is a waste of time. The more you research, the more you realize how little you actually know. Instead, you just have to do it, even if you're scared. Literature is the most agreeable way of ignoring life. The theory is for analysis; it is not for action. When you learn to draw, just draw what you see. And when we get an art education, the theory simply gives us the language to describe our tastes. The challenge I'm giving myself this year is to learn the trumpet without learning any music theory. I'm just going to play with it like a child. I'm just going to listen to a lot of music and try to make those sounds that I like. There have been enough examples of little Chinese kids being forced to learn the piano for seven years, and the moment their parents stop taking them to class, they stop playing music. I think if you love the world and think it is beautiful, learning physics, for example, would not take away from that beauty, it only adds to it. But if you study natural science without loving the natural world, you will only see the world as a machine. Which is just not that fun. You are not your work¶ When I was in art school, one thing I discovered was that many people believed they were defined by the work they created. If their work wasn't good, they saw it as a reflection of their skill rather than a reflection of their taste, which was actually superior to their ability. You are simply the person who created the work. You are a vessel, a medium for the work, but you are not the work itself. The sooner you realize this, the easier it will be to share the work you have produced, receive feedback, develop your skills, and reflect on your taste. Get Rid of Complexity¶ I think in the same way, when making plans or building systems, the same insecurity of being intelligent leads to overthinking and overcomplicating a system, in the hope that the same complexity will be a proxy for intelligence. I think this is a mistake. The best systems are the simplest ones. Feynman's approach to physics is a great example of this. Famously, Einstein said that if you can't explain something simply, you don't understand it well enough. I think this is true for many things. A Great Person is not Always a Good Person¶ Mike Tyson once said, "If you're favored by God, you're also favored by the devil." It's alright to strive for greatness, but it's equally important to choose goodness. Don't neglect your loved ones for work; time is fleeting. Appreciate people and sincerely express your love for them. Grief is the result of unexpressed love, and the key to avoiding grief is to love abundantly. Some people claim they will grind for ten years and then relax once they find a partner or something similar, but I believe that's nonsense. You won't be able to do it suddenly without practice. Along your journey of hard work, you must consciously practice winding down and being a good person. Otherwise, you'll only address it after experiencing your first divorce. Fatherhood I think fatherhood is a great example of this. I think a lot of people think that they can just be a good father when they have kids. But I think that's a mistake. I think you have to practice being a good father before you have kids. You have to practice being a good husband before you get married. It might be easy to convince myself that a good father is able to take care of the family, but how many times was that an excuse to just work yourself to death? Is the father who can pay for piano lessons truly a better man than the father who can be present at the recital? I just don't think this is something that we can turn on and off so easily. Self-Worth¶ For a large part of my life, I held onto the belief that my purpose was to help and teach people. I believed in working hard, generating capital, and taking care of my partner, family, and so on. However, when I got injured, I had to confront the fact that I wasn't able to fulfill those responsibilities. I struggled with the realization that I couldn't work, produce, or take care of my partner and family. It made me question, "If the ox cannot plow, what is it good for?" This struggle consumed me during the latter half of my twenties. Start practicing self-love. While successful people may attribute their success to their struggles, I believe a significant part of it is simply coming to terms with those struggles. Each person's pain is maximum to them. You have already endured hardships. Seek success because you deserve it and learn to love yourself, rather than punishing yourself for not being good enough. You deserve the life that you desire. I used to write down "What would I do if I really loved myself?" Sometimes that's hard, especially for someone who has been through a lot of trauma. It can be difficult to even imagine what that would look like. Instead, I now use inverted thinking. What would I do if I really hated myself? What if I wanted myself to lose out as much as possible? What would I do? Stay home all day. If my friends don't ask to hang out, don't reach out to them out of fear of rejection. Don't ask the girl out. Never learn to talk to strangers. Work hard and make tons of money but die an unhappy old man with regrets. Neglect my health and my body. Never learn to cook. Be afraid to dance in public. Make excuses and believe other people are more talented than me. Never look at how far I've come and only focus on what I don't have. Whatever it is, just do the opposite. Self Talk¶ What are the mantras you've inherited from others? How often are you chasing "The Dream" and not "Your Dream"? Really evaluate what you want and why you want it, and make sure you practice talking to yourself. Happiness is the quality of thoughts You don’t need to believe in yourself to be an artist, you just need to believe in your audience. To believe that it is important to communicate with them. Make sure that if your goal is to build an audience that you have some message to say. Otherwise it just gets hopeless. Celebrity is the cost you pay to deliver your message. Celebrity is to be hated. Celebrity is to be hated Twitter is a place where low status people can feel like high status people. I think the Internet is basically summarized by those words. For most of the people who are fighting online, the Internet might be the only place where they have a sense of control. When you approach it from a point of sympathy, even getting hate online is an opportunity to be grateful for the real-life friendships that you have, and the digital communities that you've built. Isolation Is the Gift, All Others Are a Test of Your Endurance¶ Really value your time alone. It is the only time you can truly see yourself. It is the only time you can truly hear yourself. Isolation is time not only away from people, but also media. I believe success truly comes from being yourself, but if you're always surrounded by others, the influence is too strong. But if you can truly live in isolation, you'll be like a still pond. When others project their image onto you, you'll be able to see the ripples clearly. The World Is a Mirror¶ The only language you have to describe the world is with your own perception. The world is a mirror. Often your fear of judgment might stem from your own judgment of others. Jason goes to the gym I used to be afraid of going to the gym because I was afraid of being judged. I was so focused on the idea of the right workout and the right form. If I went to the gym alone, I would just think, "Wow, people must think I'm so weak. I can barely squat the bar. I'm so lanky." I would look at the fat guy or the skinny guy and think, "Man, they are just like me, clueless." Looking back at that time, I had so much judgment of others, but it was really just a reflection of my own judgment of myself. I was so afraid of being judged, but I was the one judging myself the most. Fast forward 5 years, everyone I see coming into the gym is trying to improve their health. I don't think the same way. I look at these people and think, "Wow, they are so much braver than me. I see them coming consistently, and I can see they are getting results. Good for them! Oh, wow, they are bringing their friends. Awesome." The world looks different when you fix your own perception. You Are a Mirror¶ In the same way that the world is a mirror, you are a mirror. You are a mirror to others. When you accept this, you also realize that you are not responsible for others. The people who are negative to you are usually miserable themselves. The sooner you realize this, the easier it is to let go of the negativity. Pessimists Are Losers¶ You impress nobody except yourself when you're a pessimist. It doesn't make you a better friend. It does not make you cool and edgy. It does not make you seem smarter just because you can be critical of everything. The world is neither inherently against you nor for you, so believing one or the other is in bad faith. “He who blames others has a long way to go on his journey. He who blames himself is halfway there. He who blames no one has arrived.” Survival I think pessimism can be a valuable survival mechanism. It's the alertness that warns us of potential negative outcomes. It's a way to protect yourself. People are also more able to find problems than solutions. It's a good exercise to understand if that energy is productive, can this critique of the work improve the work? Or is it just a way to feel superior or to get sympathy? All cruelty stems from weakness, Happiness is the quality of thought Let Go of Anticipation¶ Here's a story about how regular people and Buddhist monks anticipated the future. They put both of them into an MRI and told them they were going to get shocked at some random time in the future. What they found was that regular people would have an anxious baseline response minutes before they were going to get shocked, but when they did, it was only slightly higher than the baseline. The monks, however, had no resting anxious anticipatory response. There was no overactive brain activity until the moment of the shock. But when they did feel the shock, it was much higher than the group of regular people. They felt more pain! The lesson I learned from this is that anticipation likely dampens the sensations at the moment life happens, but at the cost dampening the joy as well, while creating a contraction in the present moment. This relates to the pessimism bit. You might think you're winning because you believe the pain was lessened by expecting the worse, but you also lose because you were in anticipation the entire time. On the same token letting go of anticipation of success also allows you to enjoy the moment more, the pressure to succeed is equally a contraction. You were just a child¶ Stuck with me for a bit. I looked at all the stupid things I had done in the past, and all the things that happened to me that I didn't understand. Then someone said to me "you were just a child" and it was honestly the most liberating thing I've ever heard. I was just a child. I didn't know any better. I didn't know. You suffered for no reason. You were just a child. Now, you also have to realize that this applies to everyone else too. Its everyone's first time being a person, including your parents. Attention is a Gift¶ The most valuable thing you can give someone is your attention. Talk about the things you love, spend time with the people you love, and give your attention wisely. As I said before, grief is the result of unexpressed love, and the key to avoiding grief is to love abundantly, especially yourself. So attend to the things that matter, and make sure to spend your arrogance while you're still young. New Stuff from March 27 2026¶ Turned 32 recently, so wanted to add some more stuff here. When I first wrote this piece, I was in the middle of trying to transition back into tech after my hand injury, which you can read about in my hand injury post. A lot of what came out on the page was from those short years of self-reinvention and self-discovery, and the mantras I adopted to get through them. Below are some of the realizations I've made over the past two years as I started my business. Use Your Potions¶ I grew up super poor, and I still remember beating the first three Pokémon games without ever using a potion. I kept saving them up, thinking I would need them later, and at the end I just beat the whole fucking game. I ultimately just did a no-potion run, a no-potion challenge run of every Pokémon game, without any intention to do so. It reminded me of the purity conversations people had about Elden Ring, how it didn't count if you used magic, and it only made sense if you played with a sense of dodging and rolling and working on your timing. The game developers put magic in the game. Just as you're arguing about some kind of bullshit purity, there's someone else beating the game no-potion with Donkey Kong bongo pads. The mechanics are built into the game. All these conditions we set on ourselves are just arbitrary. Maybe I want to decide to run a company, but I refuse to raise money or hire help. Maybe I choose not to hire an EA or hire a writer. Just use your damn potions, and if you don't acknowledge that you are doing a very specific kind of challenge, run with your whole life. Allow that to be a conscious decision with your life that you wake up every day and accept. A Handful of Good Decisions¶ One of the big truths in my life. Your whole life is really built around a couple of good decisions and making as few bad decisions as possible. In business, what I learned is that focus allows you to really just make a few decisions. And oftentimes, my life is better in a year where I make one good decision and zero bad decisions. And my life is quite bad if I make five good decisions and three bad decisions. Two good decisions got me to a $3 million net worth, and one bad decision brought me down to $500,000. An even worse decision, working a little bit too much, caused me to not be able to work productively for about three years. It turns out hard work is just the entry ticket. To get to where I was, everyone works hard. Maybe the first million is hard work, but the next twenty is not just that. It's luck, leverage, and not just fucking ruining everything. After hard work, everything else is your judgment and your ability to be lucky, and I talk about that later on. Notes on All My Money Issues¶ Money has always been the anxiety¶ A lot of the past two years has actually revolved around a lot of my neuroses around money, and so I'm gonna share a lot of thoughts I've had about what money means to me, so just be warned. Not everyone likes talking about money. To give you some context, I came to the US with about $157 in my bank account. I borrowed from some friends and made a living as a machine learning researcher until my hands stopped working, and I basically thought I would never make a living again. I had a ton of issues with money growing up. It's kind of the one real issue and anxiety I have in my life. Just talk about money, it's fine¶ If you want to be successful, at some point money is going to be part of that conversation. Naturally, what you'll find is when you say numbers out loud, people start to sort themselves. They tend to reveal how they view themselves and what the value of money is. Sometimes I'll talk about money, and people will go on a forty-minute tirade on how they don't mean that money means nothing to them and they don't care about all these things. Obviously, if you're ranting to me for forty minutes because I told you that I lost a bunch of money in stocks, maybe it's actually a you thing. Because for other people, they just ask me, "What led me to make those bets?" Or do I need help with hedging or some other thing where money is seen more as just a skill? And also I personally find that if you refuse to really acknowledge money or even believe that things can be priced, the world is probably just a little bit harder for you. I remember specifically at a dinner table someone mentioned that they want to buy a $4 million house. Someone at that table just rolled their eyes and said, "That must be nice." While someone else looked at her and said, "Oh, where have you been looking? Have you figured out the fees? Do you think I'll have kids in that house? What neighborhood are you looking for? I know a real estate agent if you want." And the last time I told someone how well my business did, they just looked at me and said, "Damn Jason, I'm working just as hard as you and making a hundred times more. Let's figure out what you're doing wrong." That conversation literally led me to leave consulting and OpenAI. It turns out I was afraid of leverage, and having a better platform allowed me to access more tools and more people. And now, when people tell me how much money they make, I just think to myself, "Let's do business together sometime," and I would love to learn from you. This also reminds me of another line that Naval said once: Make money with your friends¶ I was at some event a couple years ago where someone asked Peter Thiel about the PayPal Mafia and how those friendships endured for so long. His answer was very simple: "You just got to figure out a way of making money together." Obviously, I think this is more directly aligned with the act of creation, but, man, does value creation and value capture really motivate the kind of wartime brotherhood that we have? Now, the greatest privilege I have with any friendship is to work together, but I think that has always been the case, whether it's a rock band, a startup, or even just some campaign. All I want to do is work with my friends. Do you want the rewards?¶ There's an essay I really like called Things You're Allowed to Do, and it's just this possibility space of potions. If you want to learn something, there are PhD students you can pay like $30 an hour to personally tutor you. You can just do it. Last year I saw a TikTok where a guy comes out with a bag and he's like, "Would you buy this bag for $300?" Half the people just go, "Why the fuck would I buy your bag for $300?" Other people ask what's in the bag. Turns out there's $1,000 in the bag. Life is sometimes really that simple. I feel like often times we forget to ask that question, and we only see the cost. I don't know if that fully justifies an $8,000 eight-sleep mattress, but sleeping better and living longer and having better skin and being able to show up at work feels like a pretty good investment for some people. Your future self is better than your current self, stop worrying¶ This is just a story of how stupid high school Jason was in trying to save money for future Jason. April of 2025 pulled together Japan for a friend's birthday, London for work, Paris for mine, plus too much work to move schedules cleanly. A $4,000 ticket bought sleep on the way to London and time for slides. A few weeks in Paris later, the hop to Japan for the birthday also looked brutal, so business class happened there too. A week with friends, roughly $15,000 in travel for the month, and honestly worth it because those windows don't come often. Rewind to right after high school, and again after college. Both times meant finishing school Friday and starting work Monday. Graduation trip money felt impossible at $3,000 for the whole thing, not just a flight. Between 17 and 24, skipping five weeks of trips with friends was how to "save" $6,000. That $6,000 didn't get parked in some coin that would moon, and starting jobs two weeks late wouldn't have torched anything real. Past me was just scared and didn't believe money could show up later. Future me did not need those sacrifices. How Much Does Billionaire Jason Value His Time?¶ Basically, if you do believe in yourself, you will have way more money when you are older. But then if God asks you at the age of 75 how much money you'd pay to be 28 for a week, or 32 for a month in Japan with your friends, how much would you pay? It's more than $15,000. It's more than $100,000. You'd be stupid if you didn't believe that. So just forgo it. 2024 landed around a million dollars in income, and the cleanest regret is still skipping Portugal with Nikol before the breakup and the move back to NY. Still haunts. Then the brain said an extra $80,000 that month beat a $10,000 trip, and somehow the trip felt huge. From here down is the essay pretty much as I first published it. Everything above this line, starting from the March update through Side Quests, is newer material I added on top.

评论（默认中文）

Hasz 2026/04/05 10:08
> 如何找到工作

我不知道这一点，我几乎通过冷申请获得了所有工作，包括实习机会。唯一不是这样的就是与大学里的（内部）招聘人员交谈。

不要低估这条路。我没有最好的成绩或任何东西，但（在我看来）我拥有非常适合手头工作的技能组合，并且有信心我可以应用它们。

您将在（企业）世界中与之互动的大多数人对自己的理解一无所知，并且在未知的未知领域中运作。自信，在共同已知/未知或已知/已知的事物上展示能力有很大帮助。

> How to Get a Job

Idk about this, I have gotten almost every job I have ever had on cold-apply, including internships. The only one that wasn't that way was talking to a (internal) recruiter in college.

Don't discount that path. I did not have the best grades or anything, but (IMO) a mix of skills that was a good fit for the job at hand and confidence I could apply them.

Most of the people you will interact with in the (corporate) world have no understanding of their own understanding, and are operating in unknown unknown territory. Being confident, demonstrating competence in something jointly known/unknown or known/known helps a ton.

And I am exactly the opposite - aside from my first job out of university 10 years ago I have gotten every job since through connections
在 HN 中查看
tombert 2026/04/05 13:42
我目前的工作是通过连接完成的，但之前的每一份工作都是通过将我的简历垃圾邮件发送给我什至远程合格和/或我觉得有趣的每一份工作。

我现在有了学位，但我第一次从大学退学，所以我在软件行业或任何地方都没有任何联系。当我退学时，我认为任何办公室工作的职业生涯都被排除在外了。 我一天之内就申请了阿尔迪、洛斯、汉堡王、麦当劳、星巴克和塔可钟（亲自开车申请）。

我一时兴起，几乎是自言自语，从 Craigslist 上的一则广告中申请了一份软件工作，而他们是唯一真正回复我的人，从而开启了我的软件职业生涯。我在很多不同的地方做过很多工作，尽管认识了很多有趣的人，但我只成功地将其转变为一份工作。

我不知道人们如何利用友好的关系来找到工作。

My current job was done via a connection, but every previous job has been through spamming my resume to every job I'm even remotely qualified for and/or I find interesting.

I have a degree now, but I dropped out of college the first time around, and so I didn't have any connections in the software industry, or anywhere really. When I dropped out, I assumed any desk job career was out the window. I applied to Aldi, Lowes, Burger King, McDonalds, Starbucks, and Taco Bell in one day (driving and applying in person).

On a lark, and almost as a joke to myself, I applied to exactly one software job from an ad on Craigslist, and they were the only ones who actually got back to me, thus jumpstarting my software career. I've had a lot of jobs in a lot of different places, and despite knowing lots of interesting people I've only managed to convert that to a job one time.

I have no idea how people use friendly connections to get jobs.

That's where the author is, and it's fine. IMO, these people tend to be better represented online, because getting a job through connections/influence/visibility is necessarily going to be louder than clicking submit on a form.

linkregister 2026/04/05 11:44
这很有趣。我认识的人中只有少数是通过冷申请获得工作的。我的大多数朋友要么是由同事推荐的，要么是收到了招聘人员的入站电子邮件。也就是说，除了我的计算机科学本科生之外；我们参加了大学招聘会，以进入劳动力市场。

得知冷敷方法能够取得成功真是令人振奋。

That's fascinating. I know only a handful of people who got their jobs through cold applying. The majority of my friends were either referred by colleagues or received inbound recruiter email. That is, with the exception of my cohort in CS undergrad; we attended our university career fair for out entry into the workforce.

It's heartening to know that the cold apply method can be successful.

hansvm 2026/04/05 13:11
冷申请在 IME 中效果相当好，但你必须能够完成面试才能使其有意义。我很擅长我所做的事情，我只申请适合的工作，而且我仍然只有 1-2% 的时间接受面试。 95% 的情况下我都会收到录用通知，这使得流程易于管理。

尽管我已经通过冷申请获得了 3/4 的技术工作 [0]，而且还收到了很多很多的工作机会。我知道这是可能的。

好吧，请注意，我喜欢我目前的工作，而且我永远不会通过我的网络找到类似的工作。在这方面，冷敷确实改变了游戏规则。

[0] 一种是通过 Google Foo Bar，一种是通过 Codefights（现在是 Codesignal 之类的），所以这些比冷应用稍微定制一些。

Cold applying works reasonably well IME, but you have to be able to nail the interviews for it to make sense. I'm great at what I do, I only apply to jobs which should be a good fit, and I still only get interviews 1-2% of the time. I then get offers 95% of the time, which keeps the process manageable.

I've gotten 3/4 of my tech jobs through cold applying though [0], and been offered many, many more. I know it's possible.

Ok that note, I love my current job, and I would've never found anything like it through my network. Cold applying was a literal game-changer in that regard.

[0] One was through Google Foo Bar, and one was through Codefights (now Codesignal or something), so those were slightly more tailored than cold applying.

我得到的每一份工作都是冷申请，除了 GED 高中文凭以外没有任何学位。你当然可以通过冷申请找到工作，基本上每一份参加面试的工作我都会得到一份工作机会，即使我没有担任开发人员，我也有两个工作机会我必须从中选择。我喜欢认为我的热情、知识和真正的兴趣在我的求职信和面试中得到体现。
Every job I’ve ever gotten has been cold apply, with no degree except a GED high school diploma equivalent. You can certainly get jobs through cold apply, I get a job offer for basically every job that gets to the interview, even when I hadn’t worked as a dev I had two job offers I had to pick from. I like to think my passion, knowledge, and genuine interest shines through in my cover letters and my interviews.

相同的。作为一名移民要困难得多，因为你必须比其他候选人证明更多，但我总是设法通过冷申请、零推荐找到一份工作，就连我也觉得很有趣，因为我在不同的公司有几十个人，在 LinkedIn 上有数千名粉丝，并且作为组织者在当地聚会中非常活跃，这意味着很多当地人至少知道我的名字，但每当我申请工作时仍然没有好的推荐，这就是为什么我总是诉诸冷申请。

mathisfun123 2026/04/05 11:43
> 我曾经经历过冷申请，包括实习

仅供参考，冷申请大型科技公司（例如 FAANG）就像扔掉你的申请一样。专业提示：在 LinkedIn 上 ping 人员并请求推荐。如果你是一个不错的候选人，他们会很乐意这样做，因为所有这些公司都有 O(1k) 推荐/招聘奖金。

> I have ever had on cold-apply, including internships

FYI cold-applying to bigtech (e.g., FAANG) is like throwing your application away. Pro-tip: ping people on LinkedIn and ask for a referral. If you're a decent candidate they'll happily do it because there's a O(1k) referral/hiring bonus at all of these companies.

在 HN 中查看
jongjong 2026/04/05 09:11
我对报纸图像计数实验的解释与作者的解释截然不同。

我的观点是，不幸的人不信任这个系统（有充分的理由），所以他们不信任文本；考虑到实验的性质，他们认为文本是误导他们的陷阱是合理的。它实际上完美地反映了现实，因为大多数人在所有事情上都不断被误导……但一些幸运的“被选中”的人却没有。就实验而言，这就像向不幸的人显示显示错误数字的文本，而幸运的人会看到显示正确数字的文本。这就是现实生活中实际发生的事情。

幸运的人不明白的是，仅仅生存下来，没有得到特殊的待遇，其实是很困难的，需要不断地跨越各种障碍和欺骗，而且你不能相信第三方的信息，因为每次你这样做，最终都会失去一切，或者浪费你的生命。幸运的人错误地相信了第三方信息。只有当他们停止接受特殊待遇时，他们才会知道自己错得多么离谱。然后现实却令人震惊！

给大多数人看的就是媒体想给他们看的。媒体的目的就是误导人们。只有一小部分人真正幸运地拥有导师，他们会告诉他们“媒体具有误导性，我知道，因为我影响媒体；这是现实：……”

My interpretation of the newspaper image-counting experiment is quite different from that of the author.

My view is that unlucky people don't trust the system (for a good reason) so they don't trust the text; given the nature of the experiment, it is reasonable that they would think the text is a trap to mislead them. It actually mirrors reality perfectly because most people are constantly misled about everything... But a few lucky 'chosen' people are not. In terms of the experiment it would be like showing unlucky people text which shows an incorrect number and the lucky people would see text showing the correct number. That's what's actually happening in real life.

What lucky people don't understand is that merely surviving, without receiving special treatment, is actually very difficult and it requires constantly jumping over all sorts of hurdles and deceptions and you can't afford trust third-party information because every time you did, you ended up losing everything or wasting years of your life. Lucky people are wrong to trust third-party information. They only learn how wrong they were when they stop receiving special treatment; then reality comes as a shock!

What is shown to the majority is what the media wants to show them. The media's purpose is to mislead people. Only a small handful of people are actually lucky enough to have mentors who will tell them "The media is misleading, I know because I influence the media; here is reality: ..."

在 HN 中查看
SecretDreams 2026/04/05 09:25
公平地说，不幸的人是怀疑的/悲观的/现实的，而幸运的人是天真的/乐观的？

如果是，问题是为什么？首先是什么？他们的运气还是他们的观点？也许一些锻炼的例子会在生命早期改变平衡！

Fair to say unlucky people are skeptical/pessimistic/realistic and lucky people are naive/optimistic?

If yes, the question is why? What came first? Their luck or their perspective? Maybe a couple instances of things working out tips the scales early in life!

在 HN 中查看
jongjong 2026/04/05 09:31
我很确定运气是第一位的，因为乐观/悲观是一种习得的特质。

我这样说是因为我认为自己“生性乐观，经历悲观”。

我出生在幸运的环境中，但由于我无法控制的因素，这种幸运在我十几岁时发生了转变。我亲眼目睹了它是如何运作的。

即使是现在，我仍然必须不断地控制自己并强迫自己悲观地思考……而我的悲观预测通常是正确的，或者有时不够悲观。

但我知道我是一个天生的乐观主义者，因为我不会放弃。多年来我开发了很多软件和初创公司；尽管我有意识地知道他们成功的机会为零，但我仍然在旁观他们中的大多数并保持最新状态。在内心深处，我抱有一种深深的乐观态度，相信事情将会发生变化，所有的机会都会立即到来。意识上，我知道这是妄想，但我从根本上是受情感而非思想驱动的。

开发出与其他产品非常相似（或优于）的产品，这些产品赚了数百万美元，但由于各种奇怪的人为社会政治原因而无法找到一个客户，这是一种奇怪的感觉。

I'm pretty sure the luck came first because optimism/pessimism is a learned trait.

I say this as someone who considers themselves "Optimistic by nature, pessimistic by experience."

I was born in lucky circumstances but that luck turned in my teens due to factors outside of my control. I have seen firsthand how it works.

Even now, I constantly have to catch myself and force myself to think pessimistically... And my pessimistic projections are usually right or sometimes not pessimistic enough.

But I know I'm a natural optimist by the fact that I don't give up. I've built so much software and startups over the years; most of them I'm still running on the side and keeping up to date even though I know consciously that there is zero chance they will succeed. Deep down I have a deep optimism that something will change and all the opportunities will come at once. Consciously, I know it is delusional but I'm fundamentally motivated by emotions, not thoughts.

It's a weird feeling having built products that work very similarly to (or better than) other products which rake in millions of dollars but not being able to find a single customer due to all sorts of weird contrived socio-political reasons.

在 HN 中查看
ElProlactin 2026/04/05 11:15
> 这些只是我告诉自己的谎言，让我继续真诚地生活。我并不是说这是正确的做事方式。我只是说我就是这么做的。我会尽力用我自己的经历来丰富我的建议，但我不会假装我所经历的痛苦和特权是普遍的。

有趣的是，鉴于作者意识到了这一点，他选择将其写为“建议”。他可以通过多种方式分享这些信息，而不会将其作为“建议”呈现。

> These are simply the lies I tell myself to keep on living my life in good faith. I'm not saying this is the right way to do things. I'm just saying this is how I did things. I will do my best to color my advice with my own experiences, but I'm not going to pretend that the suffering and the privilege I've experienced is universal.

It's interesting the author chose to wrote this as "advice" given his awareness of this. There are a number of ways he could have shared this information without presenting it as "advice."

vector_spaces 2026/04/05 09:15
很抱歉讨厌，但写得极其丰富

> 不要给我发送比你发送给暗恋对象更长的内容。有些人通过电子邮件给我发送六段文章，讲述他们从树上救一只猫的经历

...这是一篇杂乱无章的文章，没有充分考虑读者的感受。我知道这只是一篇博文，表面上是为作者的妹妹写的，但如果作者真的想把自己定位为一个可以接受建议的人，他应该努力让他的想法易于理解。我建议他在想法之间进行一些转变，费心做一些研究来支持他的主张，而不是例如。含糊地提到他听说的一项实验，据说涉及“幸运”和“不幸”的人（听起来确实像科学）。

看在上帝的份上，不要立即告诉我你认为我会继续阅读，更不用说仔细阅读以“注意到”你的写作中的任何内容。恶心

最后，虽然我知道在硅谷/沿海科技类型中很流行使用代理语言来证明自己对周围的人是一个无情的混蛋，但这一节的精神只有助于在少数环境中部署，通常是低复杂性的环境，其中风险很低，缺乏心理安全感，而且你迫切需要薪水。

无论如何，这里的好想法很大程度上被作者糟糕的写作和过度概括他在沿海技术工作的经验所背叛。帮自己一个忙，寻找其他榜样

I'm sorry to hate but it's extremely rich to write

> Do not send me anything longer than you would send to a crush. Some people email me six-paragraph essays about the time they saved a cat from a tree

...in a rambling piece that is not written with much consideration for the reader. I know this is just a blog post, ostensibly written for the author's younger sister, but if the author really wishes to position himself as someone to take advice from, he should make some effort to make his ideas digestible. I would suggest he include some transitions between ideas, bother to do some research to back up his claims instead of e.g. referring vaguely to an experiment he heard of supposedly involving "lucky" and "unlucky" people (truly sounds like science).

And for the love of God don't tell me right off the bat that you assume I'm going to keep reading, let alone read closely enough to "notice" anything about your writing. Yuck

Finally, while I know it's popular in Silicon Valley/coastal tech types to use the language of agency to justify being an uncharitable dick to people around you, the spirit of this particular stanza is helpful to deploy only in a small number of settings, generally low complexity environments where the stakes are low and there's a lack of psychological safety, and you desperately need the paycheck.

In any event the good ideas here are largely betrayed by the author's bad writing and overgeneralizing his experience working in coastal tech. Do yourself a favor and find other role models

jjulius 2026/04/05 10:44
>...如果作者真的希望将自己定位为听取...建议的人

>...这里的好想法很大程度上被作者糟糕的写作和过度概括所背叛...

作者显然不想成为大多数人听取建议的人，并承认这篇文章写得不好，缺乏细微差别，而且很大程度上只是对他们有用的东西，而不是其他人。我不知道当他们一开始就说得很清楚时，人们怎么可能这么认真地对待这一点：

>我真的没有资格提供建议。

>如果您正在寻求细致入微的视角，请不要阅读本文。

>这些只是我告诉自己的谎言，让我继续真诚地生活。我并不是说这是正确的做事方式。我只是说我就是这么做的。我会尽力用我自己的经历来丰富我的建议，但我不会假装我所经历的痛苦和特权是普遍的。

>... if the author really wishes to position himself as someone to take advice from...

>... the good ideas here are largely betrayed by the author's bad writing and overgeneralizing...

The author clearly doesn't want to be someone most people take advice from, and admitted that the piece wouldn't be well written, lack nuance, and largely were just things that worked for them, not anybody else. I don't know how one could possibly take this so seriously when they make it very clear up front:

>I'm not really qualified to give advice.

>Don't read this if you are seeking a nuanced perspective.

>These are simply the lies I tell myself to keep on living my life in good faith. I'm not saying this is the right way to do things. I'm just saying this is how I did things. I will do my best to color my advice with my own experiences, but I'm not going to pretend that the suffering and the privilege I've experienced is universal.
